---
title: "Transformation, Algorithms, and Working Verbs"
---

# Transformation, Algorithms, and Working Verbs

Range: `0372-0511`.

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                             | Summary                                                                                 |
| ----- | -------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0372  | [aggregate {Verb}](#rune-0372)   | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0373  | [amortize](#rune-0373)           | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0374  | [analyze](#rune-0374)            | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0375  | [annotate](#rune-0375)           | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0376  | [approximate {Verb}](#rune-0376) | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0377  | [archive](#rune-0377)            | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0378  | [assemble {Verb}](#rune-0378)    | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0379  | [batch](#rune-0379)              | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0380  | [bucket {Verb}](#rune-0380)      | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0381  | [canonicalize](#rune-0381)       | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0382  | [classify](#rune-0382)           | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0383  | [clean](#rune-0383)              | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0384  | [clone](#rune-0384)              | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0385  | [coalesce](#rune-0385)           | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0386  | [collect](#rune-0386)            | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0387  | [compact](#rune-0387)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0388  | [compare](#rune-0388)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0389  | [compile {Verb}](#rune-0389)     | A transmutation word; it transforms one language into another fit for execution.        |
| 0390  | [compress](#rune-0390)           | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0391  | [compute](#rune-0391)            | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0392  | [condense](#rune-0392)           | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0393  | [convert](#rune-0393)            | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0394  | [correlate](#rune-0394)          | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0395  | [decode](#rune-0395)             | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0396  | [decrypt {Verb}](#rune-0396)     | An unsealing word; protected meaning becomes legible again under the right key.         |
| 0397  | [deduplicate](#rune-0397)        | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0398  | [defer {Verb}](#rune-0398)       | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0399  | [delete](#rune-0399)             | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0400  | [denoise](#rune-0400)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0401  | [derive](#rune-0401)             | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0402  | [deserialize](#rune-0402)        | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0403  | [detect](#rune-0403)             | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0404  | [diff {Verb}](#rune-0404)        | A difference word; it names what changed between two states.                            |
| 0405  | [digest {Verb}](#rune-0405)      | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0406  | [dispatch {Verb}](#rune-0406)    | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0407  | [distribute](#rune-0407)         | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0408  | [drain](#rune-0408)              | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0409  | [drop](#rune-0409)               | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0410  | [encode](#rune-0410)             | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0411  | [encrypt {Verb}](#rune-0411)     | A warding word; it hides meaning from everyone except the right holder of the key.      |
| 0412  | [enumerate](#rune-0412)          | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0413  | [evaluate](#rune-0413)           | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0414  | [expand](#rune-0414)             | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0415  | [explode](#rune-0415)            | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0416  | [export {Verb}](#rune-0416)      | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0417  | [extract](#rune-0417)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0418  | [fetch](#rune-0418)              | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0419  | [filter](#rune-0419)             | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0420  | [flatten](#rune-0420)            | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0421  | [fold](#rune-0421)               | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0422  | [format](#rune-0422)             | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0423  | [fuse](#rune-0423)               | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0424  | [generate](#rune-0424)           | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0425  | [group](#rune-0425)              | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0426  | [hash {Verb}](#rune-0426)        | A condensation word; arbitrary input becomes a fixed fingerprint.                       |
| 0427  | [hoist](#rune-0427)              | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0428  | [hydrate](#rune-0428)            | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0429  | [infer](#rune-0429)              | A consequence word; it derives hidden structure from visible evidence.                  |
| 0430  | [inline {Verb}](#rune-0430)      | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0431  | [instrument](#rune-0431)         | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0432  | [integrate](#rune-0432)          | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0433  | [interpolate](#rune-0433)        | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0434  | [invert](#rune-0434)             | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0435  | [isolate {Verb}](#rune-0435)     | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0436  | [join {Verb}](#rune-0436)        | A union word; separate streams, sets, or histories are brought into one result.         |
| 0437  | [lex](#rune-0437)                | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0438  | [link](#rune-0438)               | A joining word; separate symbols are resolved into one executable body.                 |
| 0439  | [map {Verb}](#rune-0439)         | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0440  | [marshal](#rune-0440)            | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0441  | [materialize](#rune-0441)        | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0442  | [measure](#rune-0442)            | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0443  | [memoize](#rune-0443)            | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0444  | [merge {Verb}](#rune-0444)       | A reconciliation word; separate lines of change are brought back together.              |
| 0445  | [migrate](#rune-0445)            | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0446  | [minify](#rune-0446)             | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0447  | [model {Verb}](#rune-0447)       | A representative word; a simplified structure stands in for a larger reality.           |
| 0448  | [normalize](#rune-0448)          | A regularizing word; irregular forms are bent toward a standard shape.                  |
| 0449  | [optimize](#rune-0449)           | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0450  | [paginate](#rune-0450)           | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0451  | [parallelize](#rune-0451)        | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0452  | [parse](#rune-0452)              | A structure-finding word; raw symbols become an ordered tree or sequence.               |
| 0453  | [partition {Verb}](#rune-0453)   | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0454  | [patch {Verb}](#rune-0454)       | A repair word; a bounded alteration is applied to an existing body.                     |
| 0455  | [pipeline {Verb}](#rune-0455)    | A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.                                  |
| 0456  | [prepare](#rune-0456)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0457  | [preprocess](#rune-0457)         | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0458  | [profile](#rune-0458)            | A hotspot-seeking word; it reveals where time or resources are actually spent.          |
| 0459  | [project](#rune-0459)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0460  | [prune](#rune-0460)              | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0461  | [quantize](#rune-0461)           | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0462  | [query](#rune-0462)              | An asking word; it requests structure or records from stored state.                     |
| 0463  | [rasterize](#rune-0463)          | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0464  | [rebalance](#rune-0464)          | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0465  | [reconcile](#rune-0465)          | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0466  | [reconstruct](#rune-0466)        | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0467  | [reduce](#rune-0467)             | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0468  | [refactor](#rune-0468)           | A reshaping word; structure changes while intended behavior is preserved.               |
| 0469  | [reformat](#rune-0469)           | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0470  | [rehydrate](#rune-0470)          | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0471  | [reindex](#rune-0471)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0472  | [render](#rune-0472)             | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0473  | [reorder](#rune-0473)            | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0474  | [replicate](#rune-0474)          | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0475  | [reshape](#rune-0475)            | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0476  | [resolve](#rune-0476)            | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0477  | [restore {Verb}](#rune-0477)     | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0478  | [rewrite](#rune-0478)            | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0479  | [sample](#rune-0479)             | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0480  | [sanitize](#rune-0480)           | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0481  | [scan {Verb}](#rune-0481)        | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0482  | [schedule {Verb}](#rune-0482)    | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0483  | [search](#rune-0483)             | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0484  | [segment {Verb}](#rune-0484)     | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0485  | [serialize](#rune-0485)          | A flattening word; structured state is turned into a transportable sequence.            |
| 0486  | [shard {Verb}](#rune-0486)       | A splitting word; one logical body is divided across multiple partitions.               |
| 0487  | [shuffle](#rune-0487)            | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0488  | [sign {Verb}](#rune-0488)        | A sealing word; origin or integrity is bound cryptographically to content.              |
| 0489  | [smooth](#rune-0489)             | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0490  | [sort](#rune-0490)               | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0491  | [specialize](#rune-0491)         | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0492  | [split](#rune-0492)              | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0493  | [stage {Verb}](#rune-0493)       | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0494  | [stream {Verb}](#rune-0494)      | A flow word; values arrive over time rather than all at once.                           |
| 0495  | [summarize](#rune-0495)          | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0496  | [synthesize](#rune-0496)         | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0497  | [tokenize](#rune-0497)           | A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.               |
| 0498  | [trace {Verb}](#rune-0498)       | A path-of-execution word preserved for later reading.                                   |
| 0499  | [train](#rune-0499)              | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0500  | [transform](#rune-0500)          | A change word; the material remains connected to its former self while taking new form. |
| 0501  | [translate](#rune-0501)          | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0502  | [transpose](#rune-0502)          | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0503  | [truncate](#rune-0503)           | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0504  | [tune](#rune-0504)               | A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.                                |
| 0505  | [unmarshal](#rune-0505)          | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0506  | [unpack](#rune-0506)             | An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.                          |
| 0507  | [update](#rune-0507)             | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0508  | [validate {Verb}](#rune-0508)    | A gatekeeping word; inputs or outputs are checked against an expected form.             |
| 0509  | [vectorize](#rune-0509)          | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |
| 0510  | [verify](#rune-0510)             | A truth-checking verb; it demands evidence that a claim or result actually holds.       |
| 0511  | [zip](#rune-0511)                | A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.            |

## Entries

### [0372] aggregate {Verb} {#rune-0372}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0373] amortize {#rune-0373}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0374] analyze {#rune-0374}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0375] annotate {#rune-0375}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0376] approximate {Verb} {#rune-0376}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0377] archive {#rune-0377}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0378] assemble {Verb} {#rune-0378}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0379] batch {#rune-0379}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0380] bucket {Verb} {#rune-0380}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0381] canonicalize {#rune-0381}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0382] classify {#rune-0382}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0383] clean {#rune-0383}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0384] clone {#rune-0384}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0385] coalesce {#rune-0385}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0386] collect {#rune-0386}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0387] compact {#rune-0387}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0388] compare {#rune-0388}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0389] compile {Verb} {#rune-0389}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A transmutation word; it transforms one language into another fit for execution.

### [0390] compress {#rune-0390}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0391] compute {#rune-0391}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0392] condense {#rune-0392}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0393] convert {#rune-0393}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0394] correlate {#rune-0394}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0395] decode {#rune-0395}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

**Pocket gloss:** Turn encoded bytes or symbols back into usable meaning.

### [0396] decrypt {Verb} {#rune-0396}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An unsealing word; protected meaning becomes legible again under the right key.

### [0397] deduplicate {#rune-0397}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0398] defer {Verb} {#rune-0398}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0399] delete {#rune-0399}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0400] denoise {#rune-0400}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0401] derive {#rune-0401}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0402] deserialize {#rune-0402}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

**Pocket gloss:** Rebuild structured state from a stored or transmitted form.

### [0403] detect {#rune-0403}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0404] diff {Verb} {#rune-0404}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A difference word; it names what changed between two states.

**Pocket gloss:** Make change visible by showing one state against another.

### [0405] digest {Verb} {#rune-0405}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0406] dispatch {Verb} {#rune-0406}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0407] distribute {#rune-0407}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0408] drain {#rune-0408}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0409] drop {#rune-0409}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0410] encode {#rune-0410}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

**Pocket gloss:** Turn meaning into a transportable or storable representation.

### [0411] encrypt {Verb} {#rune-0411}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A warding word; it hides meaning from everyone except the right holder of the key.

### [0412] enumerate {#rune-0412}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0413] evaluate {#rune-0413}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0414] expand {#rune-0414}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0415] explode {#rune-0415}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0416] export {Verb} {#rune-0416}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0417] extract {#rune-0417}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

**Pocket gloss:** Pull the needed part out of a larger body.

### [0418] fetch {#rune-0418}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0419] filter {#rune-0419}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

**Pocket gloss:** Keep what matches; discard what does not.

### [0420] flatten {#rune-0420}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0421] fold {#rune-0421}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0422] format {#rune-0422}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0423] fuse {#rune-0423}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0424] generate {#rune-0424}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

**Pocket gloss:** Produce new structure, code, or data from rules or inputs.

### [0425] group {#rune-0425}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0426] hash {Verb} {#rune-0426}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A condensation word; arbitrary input becomes a fixed fingerprint.

### [0427] hoist {#rune-0427}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0428] hydrate {#rune-0428}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

**Pocket gloss:** Reattach lightweight structure to a previously transferred shell.

### [0429] infer {#rune-0429}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A consequence word; it derives hidden structure from visible evidence.

### [0430] inline {Verb} {#rune-0430}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0431] instrument {#rune-0431}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0432] integrate {#rune-0432}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0433] interpolate {#rune-0433}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0434] invert {#rune-0434}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0435] isolate {Verb} {#rune-0435}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0436] join {Verb} {#rune-0436}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A union word; separate streams, sets, or histories are brought into one result.

### [0437] lex {#rune-0437}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0438] link {#rune-0438}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A joining word; separate symbols are resolved into one executable body.

**Pocket gloss:** Bind separate pieces into one connected whole.

### [0439] map {Verb} {#rune-0439}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0440] marshal {#rune-0440}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

**Pocket gloss:** Pack structured state into a transportable form.

### [0441] materialize {#rune-0441}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

**Pocket gloss:** Force a deferred or virtual thing into concrete existence.

### [0442] measure {#rune-0442}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0443] memoize {#rune-0443}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0444] merge {Verb} {#rune-0444}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A reconciliation word; separate lines of change are brought back together.

**Pocket gloss:** Combine many lines or branches into one resolved form.

### [0445] migrate {#rune-0445}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

**Pocket gloss:** Move shape, meaning, or state from one regime to another.

### [0446] minify {#rune-0446}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0447] model {Verb} {#rune-0447}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A representative word; a simplified structure stands in for a larger reality.

### [0448] normalize {#rune-0448}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A regularizing word; irregular forms are bent toward a standard shape.

**Pocket gloss:** Force many possible surfaces into one agreed shape.

### [0449] optimize {#rune-0449}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

**Pocket gloss:** Trade time, space, or simplicity to improve a measured objective.

### [0450] paginate {#rune-0450}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0451] parallelize {#rune-0451}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0452] parse {#rune-0452}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A structure-finding word; raw symbols become an ordered tree or sequence.

### [0453] partition {Verb} {#rune-0453}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

**Pocket gloss:** Turn raw text into structured representation.

### [0454] patch {Verb} {#rune-0454}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A repair word; a bounded alteration is applied to an existing body.

### [0455] pipeline {Verb} {#rune-0455}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.

### [0456] prepare {#rune-0456}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0457] preprocess {#rune-0457}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0458] profile {#rune-0458}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A hotspot-seeking word; it reveals where time or resources are actually spent.

### [0459] project {#rune-0459}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0460] prune {#rune-0460}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0461] quantize {#rune-0461}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

**Pocket gloss:** Change structure to improve clarity while preserving behavior.

### [0462] query {#rune-0462}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An asking word; it requests structure or records from stored state.

### [0463] rasterize {#rune-0463}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0464] rebalance {#rune-0464}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0465] reconcile {#rune-0465}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0466] reconstruct {#rune-0466}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0467] reduce {#rune-0467}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0468] refactor {#rune-0468}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A reshaping word; structure changes while intended behavior is preserved.

### [0469] reformat {#rune-0469}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0470] rehydrate {#rune-0470}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0471] reindex {#rune-0471}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0472] render {#rune-0472}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0473] reorder {#rune-0473}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0474] replicate {#rune-0474}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0475] reshape {#rune-0475}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0476] resolve {#rune-0476}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0477] restore {Verb} {#rune-0477}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0478] rewrite {#rune-0478}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0479] sample {#rune-0479}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0480] sanitize {#rune-0480}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0481] scan {Verb} {#rune-0481}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0482] schedule {Verb} {#rune-0482}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0483] search {#rune-0483}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0484] segment {Verb} {#rune-0484}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0485] serialize {#rune-0485}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A flattening word; structured state is turned into a transportable sequence.

### [0486] shard {Verb} {#rune-0486}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A splitting word; one logical body is divided across multiple partitions.

### [0487] shuffle {#rune-0487}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0488] sign {Verb} {#rune-0488}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A sealing word; origin or integrity is bound cryptographically to content.

### [0489] smooth {#rune-0489}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

**Pocket gloss:** Write structured state into bytes, text, or wire form.

### [0490] sort {#rune-0490}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0491] specialize {#rune-0491}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0492] split {#rune-0492}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0493] stage {Verb} {#rune-0493}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0494] stream {Verb} {#rune-0494}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A flow word; values arrive over time rather than all at once.

### [0495] summarize {#rune-0495}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0496] synthesize {#rune-0496}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

**Pocket gloss:** Change form, order, or representation while preserving some intended substance.

### [0497] tokenize {#rune-0497}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A work-word; it describes how material is processed into some next state.

### [0498] trace {Verb} {#rune-0498}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A path-of-execution word preserved for later reading.

### [0499] train {#rune-0499}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0500] transform {#rune-0500}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change word; the material remains connected to its former self while taking new form.

### [0501] translate {#rune-0501}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0502] transpose {#rune-0502}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0503] truncate {#rune-0503}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0504] tune {#rune-0504}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A change-word; it alters form, order, or representation.

### [0505] unmarshal {#rune-0505}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0506] unpack {#rune-0506}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An alchemical word; it moves data from one shape into another.

### [0507] update {#rune-0507}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0508] validate {Verb} {#rune-0508}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A gatekeeping word; inputs or outputs are checked against an expected form.

### [0509] vectorize {#rune-0509}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.

### [0510] verify {#rune-0510}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A truth-checking verb; it demands evidence that a claim or result actually holds.

**Pocket gloss:** Demand evidence that a claim or result actually holds.

### [0511] zip {#rune-0511}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procedure-word; it tells software what kind of transformation is underway.
